patch.value:
## Repro (live, 2026.7.6)
PM_AUTHOR=maintainer-agent pm notes pm-doxj --add --stdin <<'EOF'
real note text
EOF
Result: note created with text '--stdin'; the heredoc content is discarded. Exit 0, no warning.

## Why it matters
Silent wrong data in the tracker (same class as GH-450 duration months bug). The agent believes the note was recorded; the actual content is lost. Because notes are append-only (no --edit/--delete, unlike comments), recovery required manual .toon surgery + history-chain revert — see companion parity task filed the same session.

## Cause
commander option --add <text> consumes the next token as its value; createStdinTokenResolver only special-cases the exact token '-'. Any other flag-like token ('--stdin', '--file', a typoed flag) becomes content.

## Suggested fix
In parseAnnotationTextInput / the shared annotation command path (src/cli/commands/annotation-command.ts, src/core/item/parse.ts), reject values matching ^--?[a-z] with a structured error that (a) shows the swallowed token, (b) lists the command's real input sources incl. the stdin spelling, (c) mentions quoting or 'text=' explicit-key form for genuinely dash-leading content. Keep an escape hatch (text=--stdin explicit key form) so legitimate dash-leading content stays writable.
Annotation --add silently stores flag-like tokens as content: pm notes <id> --add --stdin records the literal note "--stdin"

Found live during ecosystem audit #11 (2026-07-06): an agent invoked pm notes pm-doxj --add --stdin (copying comments' --stdin flag) and the CLI silently stored the string --stdin as the note text on the roadmap milestone. Option values beginning with -- are almost never intended content for annotation commands. Duplicate check 2026-07-06: pm-gdi7 covers UNKNOWN-option suggestions (different failure: here the option is known and the VALUE is a swallowed flag); pm-yql1 is facet grammar consolidation; pm-jkjt/GH-450 is the analogous silent-wrong-data class for durations; searched 'stdin', 'flag value', annotation issues — no existing item.

Annotation/value-taking flags (--add on notes/learnings, comment text sources, --message, etc.) reject or explicitly confirm values that start with '-' unless separated by '=' or preceded by '--' terminator; error names the correct stdin spelling for the command (notes/learnings: --add - ; comments: --stdin); regression tests cover notes --add --stdin, learnings --add --file, comments --add --stdin; contracts/docs updated if flag semantics change

value.note: shared annotation input parsing — guard belongs here
add-only options surface; --add value swallows flag-like tokens
createStdinTokenResolver only special-cases the exact token '-'

value.author: maintainer-agent
value.text: Commander-style value flags consume the next token even when it looks like another flag: pm notes <id> --add --stdin stores literal "--stdin" as the note and discards the heredoc, exit 0. Correct stdin spelling differs per facet (notes/learnings use '--add -'; comments has a real --stdin flag) — that asymmetry is what baits the mistake. Recovery today requires manual .toon edit + dropping the tail history entry so the prior after_hash re-anchors (validate history_drift confirms); notes/learnings have no --edit/--delete (pm-a2h3).

author: codex-cli-agent
message: Start annotation flag-like value guard for silent wrong-data prevention.

patch.value: add-only options surface; --add value swallows flag-like tokens
comment add/edit parsing threads raw value for flag-like guard

value.note: createStdinTokenResolver only special-cases the exact token '-'
comments --add --stdin rejection and stdin/text escape regression
notes/learnings flag-like --add rejection and text= escape regression

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Evidence: annotation --add values that look like flags now fail with command-specific stdin guidance while text= keeps literal dash-leading content writable. Verified comments, notes, learnings regression specs, full coverage 100/100/100/100, typecheck, lint, audit, and installed-tarball temp dogfood.

patch.value: Implemented shared annotation guard for flag-like --add values with command-specific stdin guidance and text= escape hatch; verified focused tests, full 100% coverage, type/lint/audit gates, and installed-tarball dogfood evidence.
Annotation add paths reject likely swallowed flags while keeping explicit literal dash-leading text writable.
Notes/learnings/comments reject flag-like --add values with command-specific stdin guidance, while text=--value and real stdin inputs remain accepted.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Review response: accepted Greptile P2 on implicit annotation emptyFlag handling. The flag-like --add guard now treats missing emptyFlag as the same implicit --add default used by the empty-text error path. Verification: focused annotation/register/extension command tests pass.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Review response: accepted integrated Greptile P1 on notes/learnings stdin false positives. Notes and learnings now pass rawValue=options.add into the shared annotation guard, so --add - validates the original '-' token while preserving flag-shaped stdin content. Verification: focused annotation/register/extension suite and pnpm typecheck pass.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Review response: accepted integrated Greptile P1 on comments --add - raw token. comments now passes rawValue=options.add ?? rawValue so the guard validates the original '-' token while preserving flag-shaped stdin payloads. Verification: node scripts/run-tests.mjs test -- tests/unit/commands/comments-command.spec.ts tests/unit/commands/notes-learnings-command.spec.ts tests/unit/commands/register-commands.spec.ts tests/unit/extensions/extension-command.spec.ts passed (254 tests); pnpm typecheck passed.

value.author: codex-cli-agent
value.text: Review response: accepted Greptile P2 on the unused annotation parseText input parameter. The shared parseText callback is back to a one-argument contract while rawValue remains the dedicated guard input. Verification: focused annotation/register/extension suite passed (254 tests); pnpm typecheck passed.
